Installation, Transport, Storage & Disposal
CH.09 — INSTALLATION / STORAGE / DISPOSAL
strings.content.chapter.10-warranty.title: Brand & Warranty Information
strings.content.chapter.01-safety.page.page1.blo: For your safety, please read all instructions in this manual before using this electrical appliance and always follow these safety precautions:
This product is for household use only. Do not use for any unintended purpose.
Do not allow children to play with this product or its power cord. Children and persons with limited mobility or cognitive ability should use it under adult supervision.
Small parts may pose a choking hazard. Keep away from children.
Do not use this product in high-humidity or high-temperature environments. Never use in the presence of flammable or explosive gases.
Ensure the power supply voltage matches the rating on the product nameplate.
Disconnect the power after use.
When unplugging, grip the plug itself — do not pull the power cord.
Do not immerse the unit or power cord in water or any other liquid.
The power cord must be plugged directly into a properly rated electrical outlet.
If the plug, power cord, or unit is damaged, stop using it immediately and contact the manufacturer or an authorized service center. Do not attempt self-repair.
Do not let the power cord hang over the edge of a counter — children may pull it, causing tipping or injury.
Never touch the product's electrical components with wet hands or bare feet.
The power supply connected to this product must have a residual-current device (RCD/GFCI).
The outlet must comply with local regulations. Safety regulations require mandatory grounding of this product.
Before cleaning, maintenance, or replacing parts, always disconnect the power supply.
Never insert screwdrivers or other objects into the ventilation openings.
Only authorized technicians may disassemble, inspect, or repair this product.
In case of fire, do not use water — use a CO₂ fire extinguisher.
Do not use mechanical means or any method other than those recommended by the manufacturer to accelerate defrosting.
Do not damage the refrigerant circuit.
Warning: Flammable materials — risk of fire.
Refrigerant waste must be disposed of at an authorized waste processing center. Keep away from open flames.
Do not store aerosol cans or other explosive substances containing flammable propellants inside this product.
Keep all ventilation openings clear. Keep the product away from heat sources or steam-generating appliances.
WARNING
strings.content.chapter.01-safety.page.page2.blo: Keep the product upright during transport. Tilting may cause damage.
After transport, let the product stand for 4 hours to allow the refrigerant to stabilize before powering on.
Wear gloves when servicing the motor or internal refrigeration system.
Do not block the ventilation openings to ensure proper operation.
Do not place non-food items inside the product chamber.
This product is for indoor use only.
This product is designed to make ice from drinking water. Do not use it for any other purpose.
Product noise level is below 50 dB(A).
This product's climate class is SN, suitable for ambient temperatures of 10°C–32°C / 50°F–89.6°F.
strings.content.chapter.02-usage-tips.page.page1: Place the product on a sturdy, flat, dry countertop.
Ensure at least 120 mm (4.7") ventilation clearance on all sides.
Keep away from heat sources (ovens, stovetops, etc.) and direct sunlight.
Run the cleaning cycle once before first use (see CH.08).
The first 5 ice-making cycles are a cool-down phase, so smaller ice during this stage is normal.
Use drinking water for ice making. The purer the water, the clearer the ice.
For optimal clarity, use water with low mineral content (TDS < 200 mg/L).
Suitable sources: purified water, RO-filtered water, deeply filtered water, low-TDS mineral water.
**Do NOT** use colored, flavored, carbonated, or gas-saturated beverages (tea, juice, coffee, milk, soda, etc.) — these may contaminate or damage the product.
Remove all packaging materials after unboxing, wipe the interior with a damp cloth, and dry thoroughly.
When the ice basket is full, the unit pauses automatically and alerts the user.
Only one ice size can be produced at a time, but the size can be changed during operation.
Occasional cloudy ice during continuous operation is normal and depends on water quality, water temperature, and ambient temperature.
Place the unit on a stable, level surface. Keep the rear side and right-side ventilation openings clear. Maintain at least 120 mm (4.7 in) of clearance from walls or other objects for proper airflow.
Before first use, or if the unit has not been used for a long time, run one cleaning cycle first. Refer to CH.08 Maintenance for the cleaning procedure.
Connect the power supply. The unit emits one beep, all indicators light up and then turn off, which means the unit is powered.
Press the **Power** button. The indicator lights white, which means the unit is on.
Open the lid and remove the ice basket and water tank.
Fill the bullet-ice water tank with drinking water up to the water level line.
Place the water tank back into the bullet-ice chamber. Press down on the tank by hand to make sure it is fully seated. A loose tank may prevent the unit from operating.
Place the ice basket back in and close the lid.
Press the **Make Ice** button. Switch the S / M / L indicator above the button to select the desired bullet-ice size.
After the size is selected, the unit starts making bullet ice automatically.
During ice-making, press **Make Ice** to switch the ice size.
During ice-making, press **Power** to stop the bullet-ice cycle. The ice-making indicator turns off and the unit stops making bullet ice.
When the basket is full, the **ICE FULL** indicator lights green and the unit stops making ice. After the ice melts or is removed, the unit resumes automatically.
If the **ADD WATER** indicator lights red during ice-making, the unit stops. Refill the water tank promptly.
Problem
Possible Cause
Solution
Unit won't turn on
Power not connected or outlet has no power
Check the power connection and ensure the outlet is working.
Won't drain
Drain plug not removed or drain hole blocked
Check that the drain plug at the bottom is removed; check for blockages.
Not making ice
Water tank low (ADD WATER indicator red)
Fill the water tank to the water line.
Not making ice
Mold only frosts, no ice forming (pump issue)
Fill water to the line, let stand for 30 minutes, then restart. Check through the window if water is being pumped to the tray.
Stops mid-cycle
Ice basket full (ICE FULL indicator green)
Remove ice or wait for it to melt. The unit will resume automatically.
Stops mid-cycle
Water tank low (ADD WATER indicator red)
Refill the water tank promptly. The unit will resume automatically.
First 5 batches are small
Water temperature is still high (cool-down phase)
This is normal. Ice size will increase as water temperature drops.
Poor ice quality
Insufficient ventilation or high ambient temperature
Ensure at least 120 mm clearance on all sides; move the unit to a cool, ventilated area.
Ice is not clear
High mineral content in water or temperature effects
Use purified water with TDS < 200 mg/L. Occasional opacity is normal during continuous use.
The manufacturer, distributors, and brand owners shall not be held liable for any damage or quality issues resulting from operations not in accordance with this manual.
DISCLAIMER
Troubleshooting
strings.content.chapter.08-maintenance.page.page: Fill the water tank to the water line.
With the unit powered on, press the **Clean** button. The indicator lights white and the unit cleans automatically.
When cleaning is complete, the unit stops automatically and the indicator turns off.
Unplug the power cord and place the unit near a sink.
Remove the drain plug from the bottom of the unit and let all water drain out.
Once drained, replace the drain plug securely.
Regularly rinse the interior with clean water — we recommend **cleaning before each daily use**.
Wipe the exterior with a soft, damp cloth. Do not use abrasive or corrosive cleaners.
Follow the auto-clean steps to clean the ice mold and tray.
Always disconnect power before maintenance and wait for the unit to cool down.
strings.content.chapter.09-installation-storage-: Packaging & Transport
Keep the unit balanced during transport to prevent tipping. The tilt angle must not exceed 45°.
Always keep the product upright during handling and transport to avoid compressor or refrigerant-system damage.
After transportation, let the unit stand upright for 4 hours before powering on.
Installation
Install the product on a flat, stable, and dry surface. Do not operate it while tilted.
Do not drag or force the product during positioning to avoid tipping or component damage.
Keep at least 120 mm (4.7") of clearance from walls and other objects for ventilation and service access.
Keep the product away from heat sources, steam, and direct sunlight.
Storage & Disposal
Before long-term storage, drain all water from the tank and inner cavity, then clean and dry the unit thoroughly.
Store the unit in a dry, ventilated environment and avoid heavy loads or moisture exposure.
This product contains electronic components and R600a refrigerant and must not be disposed of with household waste.
At end of life, contact an authorized recycling center or retailer and follow local disposal regulations.
Do not damage the refrigerant circuit. End-of-life treatment must be handled by a qualified recycling or waste-management service to avoid environmental or safety risks.
WEEE / R600a
Installation, Transport, Storage & Disposal
strings.content.chapter.10-warranty.page.page1.b:
Thank you for purchasing the {{brand.display_name}} {{localized.product_name}}. If you have any questions, please contact us at:
{{brand.support_email}} | {{brand.website}}

This product comes with a **{{warranty.years}}-year limited warranty**. Warranty replacement service is available within {{warranty.years}} year(s) from the original date of purchase. To be eligible for warranty replacement, you must:
Have purchased the product within the past {{warranty.years}} year(s).
Have the original order number or the email address used at the time of purchase.
If purchased through a dealer or retail partner, provide a copy of the original receipt.
Send us an email to request warranty service: **{{brand.support_email}}**
